description: Overhaul the build/dev/check command surface to be minimal, intuitive, and correct. Reduce 28 scripts to ~12, fix the dev --dev flag bug, make type-check a true noEmit check, remove double-work in check, parallelize independent build steps, speed up pre-commit, and standardize conventions across platform/docs/plugins.

userStories.title: Fix dev orchestrator missing --dev flag for MCP server
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want `bun run dev` to start the MCP server in dev mode so that dev-mode features (file watchers, config watching, hot reload) work correctly in full dev mode.
acceptanceCriteria: In scripts/dev.ts, the MCP server spawn command includes '--dev' as a CLI argument (after the script path, not before)
Running `bun run dev` starts the MCP server with --dev (visible in the spawn args)
The existing behavior of dev:mcp ('bun --hot platform/mcp-server/dist/index.js --dev') is unchanged
userStories.notes:
In scripts/dev.ts:263, the MCP server is spawned as:

```ts
const mcp = Bun.spawn(['bun', '--hot', 'platform/mcp-server/dist/index.js'], {
```

But it should be:

```ts
const mcp = Bun.spawn(['bun', '--hot', 'platform/mcp-server/dist/index.js', '--dev'], {
```

The `--dev` flag must come AFTER the script path (it's a script argument, not a bun argument). Compare with the dev:mcp script in root package.json which correctly has `--dev` at the end. This is a one-line fix.

userStories.title: Make type-check a true --noEmit check and fix check pipeline
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want `type-check` to be a fast non-emitting check and `check` to not do double work, so that the verification pipeline is correct and fast.
acceptanceCriteria: The root type-check script runs 'tsc --build --noEmit' (no file emission, fast verification only)
The root check script runs build first, then lint, format:check, knip, and test — but NOT type-check (because build already type-checks via tsc --build)
The root build script uses 'tsc --build' (incremental by default, no --force) for faster rebuilds
A new 'build:force' script exists for full clean rebuilds: 'tsc --build --force && ...' (the current build behavior)
userStories.notes:
Current state in root package.json:
- `build`: `tsc --build --force && ...` (always full rebuild — slow)
- `type-check`: `tsc --build` (incremental, but EMITS files — not a true type-check)
- `check`: runs both `build` AND `type-check` back-to-back (double work)

Target state:
- `build`: `tsc --build && ...` (incremental by default — fast for iterative development)
- `build:force`: `tsc --build --force && ...` (full rebuild for CI or troubleshooting only)
- `type-check`: `tsc --build --noEmit` (fast, no file emission, pure verification — note: --noEmit with --build requires TypeScript 5.x+ which is already in use)
- `check`: `bun run build && bun run lint && bun run format:check && bun run knip && bun run test` (build already type-checks, so type-check is removed from the pipeline)

IMPORTANT: When changing `build` from `--force` to incremental, keep the full extension pipeline chain: `tsc --build && bun run --cwd platform/browser-extension build:bundle && bun run --cwd platform/browser-extension build:side-panel && bun run build:icons && bun run build:install-extension`.

Also verify that `tsc --build --noEmit` works correctly with the project references setup in tsconfig.json. Run it manually to confirm.

userStories.title: Consolidate and clean up root package.json scripts
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want a clean, minimal set of top-level scripts so that I can quickly understand and use the available commands without cognitive overload.
acceptanceCriteria: The 'build:tsc' script is removed (redundant with 'build' and the new 'build:force')
The 'test:unit' script is removed (exact duplicate of 'test')
The 'build:e2e-plugin' script is removed from top-level; its command is inlined into 'test:e2e'
The 'build:icons' script is removed from top-level; it is only called internally by 'build' and scripts/dev.ts
The 'build:install-extension' script is removed from top-level; it is only called internally by 'build' and scripts/dev.ts
The 'check:tsconfig-coverage' script is removed from top-level (it remains usable via 'bun scripts/check-tsconfig-coverage.ts' directly)
After removing scripts, update the build script to inline the icon generation and install-extension commands (since they can no longer be referenced as 'bun run build:icons')
The build script chain uses direct 'bun scripts/generate-icons.ts' and 'bun scripts/install-extension.ts' calls instead of 'bun run build:icons' and 'bun run build:install-extension'
scripts/dev.ts does NOT reference any removed script names (verify it calls extension build steps directly, which it already does)
userStories.notes:
The goal is to reduce the script surface from 28 to ~12-14 meaningful commands. Remove these scripts from root package.json:

1. `build:tsc` — redundant with `build` (which starts with tsc) and the new `build:force`
2. `test:unit` — exact alias of `test`
3. `build:e2e-plugin` — inline into `test:e2e`: change `test:e2e` to `cd plugins/e2e-test && bun install --frozen-lockfile && bun run build && bunx playwright test`
4. `build:icons` — internal detail, called by build chain and dev.ts. In the build chain, replace `bun run build:icons` with `bun scripts/generate-icons.ts`
5. `build:install-extension` — internal detail. In the build chain, replace `bun run build:install-extension` with `bun scripts/install-extension.ts`
6. `check:tsconfig-coverage` — maintenance utility, not a daily workflow. Developers can run `bun scripts/check-tsconfig-coverage.ts` directly

After removal, the build script becomes:
```
tsc --build && bun run --cwd platform/browser-extension build:bundle && bun run --cwd platform/browser-extension build:side-panel && bun scripts/generate-icons.ts && bun scripts/install-extension.ts
```

IMPORTANT: Verify scripts/dev.ts does not depend on any of the removed script names. Looking at dev.ts, it calls the extension build steps directly via Bun.spawn (not via `bun run build:icons`), so it should be unaffected. But double-check by reading the file.

Also check if knip.ts or any other config references these script names.

userStories.title: Unify check commands and rename check:all to check:everything
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want clear naming for verification commands so that I know exactly what each check covers without guessing.
acceptanceCriteria: The 'check' script runs the platform verification suite: build + lint + format:check + knip + test (the daily driver for platform developers)
The 'check:all' script is renamed to 'check:everything' and runs: bun run check && bun run test:e2e && bun run check:docs && bun run check:plugins (the CI/pre-release full suite)
The 'check:docs' script remains but is simplified to just 'bun run --cwd docs check' (delegates to docs' own check script instead of duplicating the command list)
bun run check:everything passes (or at least the command structure is correct)
userStories.notes:
Current state:
- `check`: `bun run build && bun run type-check && bun run lint && bun run format:check && bun run knip && bun run test` (type-check is double work after build)
- `check:all`: `bun run build && bun run type-check && bun run lint && bun run format:check && bun run knip && bun run test && bun run test:e2e && bun run check:docs && bun run check:plugins`
- `check:docs`: `bun run --cwd docs build && bun run --cwd docs type-check && bun run --cwd docs lint && bun run --cwd docs knip && bun run --cwd docs format:check` (duplicates what `docs/package.json` check script already does)

Target state:
- `check`: `bun run build && bun run lint && bun run format:check && bun run knip && bun run test` (no type-check since build already does it)
- `check:everything`: `bun run check && bun run test:e2e && bun run check:docs && bun run check:plugins`
- `check:docs`: `bun run --cwd docs check` (delegates to docs' own check script)
- `check:plugins`: stays as is (runs scripts/plugins.ts --check)

Also update CLAUDE.md references to check:all → check:everything. Search for 'check:all' in:
- Root CLAUDE.md
- Root package.json (the script itself)
- .ralph/__SKILL__.md (references default suite)
- Any other files that mention check:all

IMPORTANT: The ralph skill file (.ralph/__SKILL__.md) should NOT be modified. It references the default verification suite generically — check if it mentions 'check:all' by name.

userStories.title: Remove knip from pre-commit hook
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want fast git commits so that the pre-commit hook doesn't slow me down with a full-project unused-code scan on every commit.
acceptanceCriteria: The .husky/pre-commit file no longer runs 'bun run knip'
The .husky/pre-commit file still runs lint-staged (formatting + linting of staged files)
The .husky/pre-commit file still has the ralph state file guard
knip still runs as part of 'bun run check' (so it's not lost, just moved out of the commit hook)
userStories.notes:
Current .husky/pre-commit:
```bash
# Reject ralph state files — these are gitignored ephemeral working files
if git diff --cached --name-only | grep -qE '\.ralph/(prd\.json|progress\.txt)$'; then
  echo "ERROR: Ralph state files must not be committed:"
  git diff --cached --name-only | grep -E '\.ralph/(prd\.json|progress\.txt)$'
  echo ""
  echo "Run: git rm --cached <file> to untrack them."
  exit 1
fi

bunx lint-staged --allow-empty
bun run knip
```

Just remove the last line (`bun run knip`). Knip is a project-wide analysis tool that scans all files — it's not scoped to staged changes like lint-staged is. Running it on every commit adds significant latency (often 5-15 seconds) with no targeted benefit. It belongs in `bun run check` (where it already is) and in CI, not in the commit hook.

userStories.title: Add safety guard to dev:mcp for missing dist/
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want `bun run dev:mcp` to give a clear error if dist/ is missing, so that I'm not confused by cryptic runtime errors when I haven't built yet.
acceptanceCriteria: The dev:mcp script checks for platform/mcp-server/dist/index.js before starting bun --hot
If the file is missing, it prints a clear message like 'Error: dist/ not found. Run `bun run build` first.' and exits with code 1
If the file exists, it proceeds normally with bun --hot
The implementation is a small wrapper script (e.g., scripts/dev-mcp.ts) that the dev:mcp script calls

userStories.title: Update CLAUDE.md to reflect the new command surface
userStories.description: As a platform developer (or AI agent reading CLAUDE.md), I want the documentation to accurately reflect the new commands so that I don't run obsolete or incorrect commands.
acceptanceCriteria: The Commands section in root CLAUDE.md reflects the actual scripts in package.json after all previous stories
Removed scripts (build:tsc, test:unit, build:icons, build:install-extension, build:e2e-plugin, check:tsconfig-coverage) are no longer listed
check:all references are updated to check:everything
The 'type-check' description clarifies it's a fast --noEmit check (no file emission)
The 'build' description clarifies it's incremental by default
The Verification section still lists the correct commands to run (build, type-check, lint, knip, test, test:e2e)
No references to removed scripts remain in CLAUDE.md
userStories.notes:
Read the root CLAUDE.md and update the Commands section and any other references to match the new script surface.

The Commands section currently lists:
```
bun run build           # Build all packages
bun run build:tsc       # TypeScript only
bun run build:icons     # Generate extension icons
bun run build:plugins   # Build all plugins
bun run build:docs      # Build docs site
bun run dev             # Full dev mode
bun run dev:mcp         # MCP server only with hot reload
bun run dev:docs        # Docs dev server
bun run check           # Root checks
bun run check:all       # Everything
bun run check:docs      # Docs checks
bun run check:plugins   # Plugin checks
bun run type-check      # TypeScript check
bun run lint            # ESLint check
bun run lint:fix        # ESLint auto-fix
bun run format          # Prettier format
bun run format:check    # Prettier check
bun run knip            # Unused code detection
bun run test            # Unit tests
bun run test:unit       # Alias for test
bun run test:e2e        # E2E tests
bun run clean           # Remove build artifacts
bun run clean:all       # clean + remove node_modules
bun run storybook       # Launch Storybook
```

After changes, it should list only the scripts that actually exist, organized clearly. Also update the Verification section which references 'check:all'.

Search for 'check:all' and 'build:tsc' and 'test:unit' throughout CLAUDE.md to find all references.
